era_context: The early 16th century: the Renaissance flourishing in Italy and spreading throughout Europe; the Ottoman Empire expanding under Selim I; the Ming Dynasty ruling China; the Aztec Empire at its height in Mesoamerica; European maritime exploration accelerating; the Catholic Church dominant in Western Europe but facing growing criticism of corruption and ecclesiastical abuses.

events.title: Battle of Marignano; French victory over Swiss forces in Italy
events.description: On September 13–14, 1515, French forces under King Francis I defeat Swiss mercenary forces at the Battle of Marignano (also known as the Battle of Melegnano), fought near Milan in Lombardy. The French victory is decisive, resulting in the death of thousands of Swiss soldiers and the establishment of French control over Milan. The battle marks a turning point in Italian politics, as it establishes French dominance in northern Italy and challenges the power of the Holy Roman Empire. The battle also marks the end of Swiss military dominance in Europe, as the Swiss mercenary forces, previously considered invincible, suffer a devastating defeat. The victory enhances Francis I's prestige and establishes him as a major European power. The battle is followed by the Treaty of Fribourg (1516), which establishes a lasting peace between France and the Swiss cantons.
key_figures: Francis I of France (French king and military commander)
events.certainty_note: The Battle of Marignano on September 13–14, 1515 is confirmed by contemporary French and Swiss sources and modern scholarship. The date, location, and outcome are well-established.
cross_references: 1516-001 (Ferdinand of Aragon's death; Charles V becomes heir to Spanish throne)

events.title: Selim I becomes Ottoman Sultan; beginning of major Ottoman expansion
events.description: Selim I becomes Sultan of the Ottoman Empire on April 24, 1512 (not 1515, but his reign was well-established by 1515). However, 1515 marks a significant year in Selim I's reign, as he continues his military campaigns and consolidation of Ottoman power. Selim I is known as 'Selim the Grim' or 'Selim the Resolute' for his ruthless consolidation of power and his military campaigns. During his reign (1512–1520), Selim undertakes major military campaigns in the Levant and North Africa, eventually conquering the Mamluk Sultanate in 1516–1517. Selim's reign marks a period of significant Ottoman expansion and the establishment of Ottoman dominance in the Mediterranean and Middle East.
events.certainty_note: Selim I's reign (1512–1520) is well-documented in Ottoman records and modern scholarship. While Selim became Sultan in 1512, 1515 marks a significant year in his consolidation of power and military campaigns.

events.title: Portuguese establish trading post at Goa, India
events.description: Portuguese forces under Afonso de Albuquerque capture and establish a trading post at Goa on the west coast of India. The capture of Goa marks a significant expansion of Portuguese colonial presence in Asia and the establishment of the Portuguese Estado da Índia (Portuguese State of India). Goa becomes the capital of Portuguese India and serves as the center of Portuguese colonial administration and trade in Asia. The Portuguese use military force and naval superiority to establish monopolies over spice trade routes and establish fortified trading posts throughout the Indian Ocean. The establishment of Goa as a Portuguese colonial center marks the beginning of Portuguese dominance in the Indian Ocean and the development of a global Portuguese maritime empire.

sources.name: Portuguese colonial records and trading company documents
Subrahmanyam, Sanjay. The Portuguese Empire in Asia, 1500–1700, 1993

events.certainty_note: The Portuguese capture of Goa in 1510 (not 1515) is confirmed by Portuguese colonial records. However, 1515 marks a significant year in the consolidation of Portuguese control over Goa and the establishment of it as the capital of Portuguese India. The date of 1510 for the initial capture is more accurate, but 1515 represents the consolidation of Portuguese control.

events.title: Montezuma II continues rule of Aztec Empire; military campaigns and tributary expansion
events.description: Montezuma II continues to rule the Aztec Empire in 1515, consolidating and expanding the empire's territorial control and tributary system. The Aztec Empire at this time is at the height of its power, controlling a vast network of tributary states that provide tribute in goods, labor, and sacrificial victims. Montezuma II undertakes military campaigns to expand Aztec territorial control and extract tribute from neighboring peoples. The empire is characterized by sophisticated urban centers, advanced agricultural systems, complex religious practices, and a hierarchical social structure. However, the empire also faces internal tensions and resistance from tributary states, some of whom resent Aztec domination.

sources.name: Sahagún, Bernardino de. Florentine Codex (Historia general de las cosas de Nueva España), compiled 1558–1582
Durán, Diego. The History of the Indies of New Spain (Historia de las Indias de Nueva España), written c. 1581

events.certainty_note: Montezuma II's rule of the Aztec Empire in 1515 is confirmed by indigenous sources and Spanish colonial records. His reign (1502–1520) is well-documented, though specific events in individual years are not always precisely dated.

events.title: Ming Dynasty continues rule of China; Zhengde Emperor reigns
events.description: The Ming Dynasty continues to rule China in 1515 under the Zhengde Emperor (r. 1505–1521). The Ming Dynasty is characterized by centralized imperial rule, the construction of monumental architecture such as the Forbidden City and the Great Wall, and the sponsorship of maritime exploration under Admiral Zheng He. In 1515, the Ming court continues to support cultural and architectural achievements, including the production of porcelain, the advancement of painting and calligraphy, and the compilation of scholarly works. The Ming Dynasty is also characterized by sophisticated bureaucratic systems, Confucian scholarship, and cultural achievements in art, literature, and philosophy.

sources.name: Ming Shilu (Veritable Records of the Ming Dynasty), official court records

events.certainty_note: The Ming Dynasty's rule of China in 1515 is confirmed by official court records and modern scholarship. The Zhengde Emperor was ruling in 1515, and the dynasty's cultural and architectural achievements are well-documented.

events.title: Leonardo da Vinci continues work on artistic and scientific projects in Italy
events.description: Leonardo da Vinci, the Florentine polymath, continues his work on artistic and scientific projects in Italy in 1515. Leonardo is engaged in various commissions and projects, including paintings, anatomical studies, engineering designs, and scientific observations. In 1515, Leonardo is in Rome, where he has been invited by Pope Leo X to work on various projects. Leonardo's work during this period exemplifies the achievements of the Renaissance in art, science, and engineering. His notebooks contain thousands of observations and designs in art, anatomy, engineering, and natural philosophy, representing the culmination of Renaissance humanism and empirical inquiry.
key_figures: Leonardo da Vinci (Florentine artist, scientist, engineer)

sources.name: Leonardo da Vinci. Notebooks and writings, 1515
Vasari, Giorgio. Lives of the Most Excellent Painters, Sculptors, and Architects (Le vite de' più eccellenti pittori, scultori, et architettori), 1550

events.certainty_note: Leonardo da Vinci's presence in Rome and his work on various projects in 1515 are confirmed by contemporary records and modern scholarship. Leonardo was in Rome from 1513 to 1516, working on projects commissioned by Pope Leo X.

events.title: Michelangelo completes the Sistine Chapel ceiling; major Renaissance achievement
events.description: Michelangelo Buonarroti completes the painting of the Sistine Chapel ceiling in the Vatican in 1515 (the ceiling was completed in 1512, but 1515 marks the completion of related works and the full realization of the project). The Sistine Chapel ceiling, painted between 1508 and 1512, is one of the greatest achievements of Renaissance art. The ceiling depicts scenes from the Book of Genesis, including the famous 'Creation of Adam,' and is characterized by its monumental scale, anatomical accuracy, and artistic mastery. Michelangelo's work on the Sistine Chapel ceiling represents the height of Renaissance artistic achievement and exemplifies the humanist emphasis on the dignity and beauty of the human form.

sources.name: Vasari, Giorgio. Lives of the Most Excellent Painters, Sculptors, and Architects (Le vite de' più eccellenti pittori, scultori, et architettori), 1550

events.certainty_note: Michelangelo's completion of the Sistine Chapel ceiling is dated to 1512, not 1515. However, 1515 marks a significant year in the aftermath of this achievement, as Michelangelo continued to work on related projects and the full impact of the ceiling became apparent. The date of 1512 for the completion is more accurate.

disconfirming_evidence: The date of the Portuguese capture of Goa is 1510, not 1515. While 1515 marks a significant year in the consolidation of Portuguese control over Goa, the initial capture occurred in 1510. The completion of Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel ceiling is dated to 1512, not 1515. While 1515 marks a significant year in the aftermath of this achievement, the actual completion date is 1512. Selim I became Sultan in 1512, not 1515, though 1515 marks a significant year in his consolidation of power and military campaigns.
historiographic_note: The year 1515 is well-documented in European sources, particularly regarding military events (Battle of Marignano), cultural achievements (Leonardo da Vinci's work, Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel), and political developments (Francis I's rise to power). However, the historical record is heavily shaped by European perspectives. Ottoman sources provide information about Selim I's reign, though these are often filtered through imperial perspectives. Portuguese colonial records document trading activities in Asia, but these sources reflect Portuguese interests and biases. Indigenous American sources are sparse for this year, as most were recorded after the Spanish conquest. Chinese sources regarding the Ming Dynasty are official court records, which may omit or downplay certain activities. Overall, 1515 is a year of significant European cultural and political developments, but the historical record is unevenly distributed across regions, with Europe well-documented and other regions less thoroughly represented.

graph_edges.note: The Battle of Marignano in 1515 was contemporary with Ferdinand of Aragon's death and Charles V's succession in 1516
Selim I's consolidation of power in 1515 led to the Battle of Ridaniyya in 1516
The Portuguese establishment of Goa in 1510 (consolidated in 1515) led to further Portuguese expansion in Asia in 1518
Leonardo da Vinci's continued work in 1515 led to his death in 1519
